suggestedPatch.target: Planning Oahu for a small group works best when you combine tight decision-making early with flexible low-energy starts.
suggestedPatch.before: Planning Oahu for a small group works best when you combine tight decision-making early with flexible low-energy starts.
suggestedPatch.after: Plan hard early, then leave mornings loose—that's the trick for groups of six who all want different things.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Mix of human-sounding jabs and SEO-committee filler; the takeaways section reads corporate.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has some real opinions (skip Hanauma if you miss the booking, Ala Moana beats Waikiki) but buries them in generic advice.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — A few good lines (malasadas, poke twice) but most phrasing is predictable travel-blog cadence.

suggestedPatch.target: Knowing these cycles minimizes group drama about weather or prices.
suggestedPatch.before: Knowing these cycles minimizes group drama about weather or prices.
suggestedPatch.after: Tell the group it might rain in February now, before someone books a non-refundable surf lesson and blames you.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: A few good lines (malasadas, poke twice) but most phrasing is predictable travel-blog cadence.
